priorities.check_method: Full deep-read audit (Fable 5 Ultracode), research-only, NO production code changed. Deep-read the entire core sim: physics/ (verlet, bonds, collision, spatial, physicsLoop), state/ (world dispatch seam, save/netSnapshot, scoring complexity-income, territory engulf-sag, gameMode, placePrimitive, sparkLifecycle, benchGate, disruptionManager, rainbow derangement, stateHash, rng), net/ (protocol v14, sync HostSync/ClientSync render-delay buffer, hostHandlers/clientHandlers trust chain, successionWarrant D1), combos.ts (36-entry table, 14 magic order-symmetric), main.ts tick loop. Smell sweeps (TODO/ts-ignore/skip/console/Math.random/Date.now/setInterval), ts-prune dead-export pass, npm outdated. 3-way Council (Grok-4.20-reasoning ANALYST + Gemini-2.5-pro AUDITOR — FIRST full 3-way since S111) on 8 ranked findings + PRIME-AUDIT of every Council-surfaced risk against code. Deliverable AUDIT_S116.md: 11 findings ranked severity×effort. TOP-3 (both models converged): F1 per-tick redundant complexity/component recompute on host (High/Small — no memoization, ~12+ graph walks/tick at 6P; Grok called it understated), F7 host-migration+worker-sim half-built (High/Large — host death kills match), F8 geometric-builder core under-delivered (High/Med — 14/36 placeholders, 9/14 magic combos visuals-only; reframed from arch-bug to product-debt per Grok). PRIME-AUDIT REFUTED: sim RNG nondeterminism (only Math.random is render-only sudoku fireworks; hazards use seeded mulberry32), full-vs-incremental snapshot divergence (NetSnapshot IS full-every-10Hz), golden-replay-in-CI (save.replay.test.ts 24 tests in suite). CONFIRMED gaps: F9 no INTENT rate-limit, F10 Pixi-leak UNVERIFIED (logged not dropped). Recommended queue: Tier-A A1 F1-memoization + A2 consistency/hygiene batch (F3/F4/F5/F6) THIS session-class (safe/high-value); Tier-B strategic fork (B1 hostmig-D2 [parked PDR] vs B2 worker-sim vs B3 one symbiotic combo) as owner pick. gates untouched (research: tsc 0, vitest 1779/1779 baseline held).

Defers F1b (territory global-labeling) to carry-forward per Council risk-isolation.
priorities.user_approval_verbatim: approved to run per your recommendations
priorities.check_method: F1a shipped (471105d). scoring.computeComplexity re-walked world.primitives+world.bonds once PER PLAYER inside tickScoring (O(P*(prims+bonds))/tick on host). NEW computeAllComplexities(world): one prim pass (bucket primCount+owned-spawner by placedBy, skip fouled) + one bond pass (bucket magic/functional/filament by aId-owner with IDENTICAL undefined/fouled skips) + per-player IDENTICAL one-shot final expression (primCount*PRIM_WEIGHT + magicBonds*MAGIC_BONUS + min(fb,floor(1.5*pc))*FUNCTIONAL_BOND_COMPLEXITY + filament*FILAMENT_INCOME_COMPLEXITY + spawner*SPAWNER_INCOME_COMPLEXITY). computeComplexity is now a thin wrapper over it (single source of truth — Gemini mandate; single-shot callers gameState WIN-log + debugOverlay unchanged). tickScoring computes the map ONCE. BYTE-IDENTICAL BY CONSTRUCTION: integer counts (order-independent) + verbatim expression = no incremental float accumulation to reorder (refutes Grok's IEEE-order REJECT — the old code also counted ints then multiplied once). PRIME-AUDIT of Council split: Grok said ship-F1b-not-F1a (float REJECT), Gemini said ship-F1a-not-F1b (canonical wrapper + Object.is test); Supervisor shipped F1a (byte-identical by construction once Grok's misread corrected) + deferred F1b. GATE: tsc 0; new scoring.differential.test.ts (600+200 random worlds, seated+non-seated ids 0..7, cap-boundary — bit-exact via .toBe()/Object.is) PASS; full vitest 1779->1782 (+3); ALL 24 save.replay byte-identity tests green; PROTOCOL_VERSION 14 held. RALPH:PATROL PASS. CARRY-FORWARD (logged): F1b territory union-find/placerColor-labeling + F2 placement radius reuse.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S117-P1 #prime-audit-the-council-not-just-the-code: Grok REJECTed F1a on an IEEE-754 float-summation-order kill-shot (N*0.6 vs incremental +=0.6 in Map order) — a real hazard IN GENERAL, but PRIME-AUDIT against the actual code refuted it: computeComplexity counts INTEGERS (primCount++/magicBonds++) then does the float math ONCE in a single final expression; there is no incremental float accumulation to reorder. Keeping that final expression VERBATIM + feeding identical integer buckets makes the single-pass version byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION. LESSON: a Council kill-shot is a hypothesis to verify against the code, not a verdict to obey — the strongest adversary can still misread the implementation; the code is the arbiter.

S117-P1 #ship-the-byte-identical-half-defer-the-subtle-half: the audit's F1 had two collapses — F1a (scoring, integer-count-then-multiply-once → byte-identical by construction) and F1b (territory, needs global component-labeling that must replicate the exact placerColor-relative connectivity rule). Council split on which to ship; the resolution was to ship the provably-safe half (F1a) now and defer the subtle half (F1b) to its own increment with its own union-find + differential test. LESSON: when a perf refactor has a trivially-equivalent part and a partition-equivalence part, split them — the differential-test harness built for the safe half de-risks the subtle half later.
